mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals imminent labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, supporting corporate revenue projections and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative forward return in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when either series retracts.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which translates to higher corporate output and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 305.0 confirms entrenched inflationary pressures, forcing markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates indicate a healthy labor market without overheating, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when tested against a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings growth, providing a fundamental floor that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows a negative or null correlation with 60-day forward returns, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals a severe supply-side contraction or demand collapse, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
